sourceMovie.title: U.S. Marshals
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto U.S. Marshals (1998) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Con Air
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If U.S. Marshals clicked for you, Con Air (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after U.S. Marshals?
faq.answer: Try Con Air, Once Upon a Time in Mexico, Spy Game—each shares DNA with U.S. Marshals while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is U.S. Marshals?
faq.answer: U.S. Marshals (1998) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is U.S. Marshals part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Marshal Samuel Gerard Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
